title: aesculus pavia var. discolor
intro: Aesculus pavia var. discolor, also known as the Red Buckeye, is a variety of flowering tree native to the southeastern United States. The Red Buckeye has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of buckeye, a toxic alkaloid that can also be used as a purgative. The tree's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Red Buckeye is often used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and salves, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
